sections.title: The passive (present simple, past simple, will)

content.subtitle: Form
content.description: be in the right form + past participle

content.subtitle: Active and passive
items: present simple: They always organise school trips. → School trips **are** always **organised**.
past simple: They repaired the road yesterday. → The road **was repaired** yesterday.
will: They **will** publish the results tomorrow. → The results **will be published** tomorrow.

content.subtitle: Use
items: When the person responsible for the action is unknown: My laptop **was stolen** last week.
When the person responsible for the action is unimportant or obvious: **Was** the homework **checked** today?

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.intro: If you are unsure how to form a passive sentence, start by thinking of the **active** sentence first.
items: Look at the active sentence. The verb is *broke* and the object is *the classroom window*.
The object of the active sentence (*the classroom window*) becomes the subject of the passive sentence. *The classroom window* …
Then we need the verb *be* in the same tense as the verb in the active sentence. Here, *broke* is past simple, so we need *was*. *The classroom window was* …
Then we need the past participle of the verb in the active sentence. The past participle of *break* is *broken*. *The classroom window was broken* …
Finally, we finish the sentence in the right way. ***The classroom window was broken yesterday.***

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: When the verb in a passive sentence is a phrasal verb, don't forget to include the particle. They **turned off** the lights. → The lights were **turned off**.
Some verbs have irregular past participle forms (e.g. break → broken).

sections.title: A: Complete with the correct passive form
sections.instructions: Use the verbs in brackets in the present simple passive, past simple passive or will passive.

items.sentence: The school uniforms ...... (wash) every week.
The concert hall ...... (decorate) for the festival last month.
The exam results ...... (email) tomorrow morning.
These phones ...... (produce) in South Korea.
The reports ...... (not finish) yesterday.
...... the prizes ...... (give) after the competition?
All essays ...... (mark) by our English teacher.
My keys ...... (not discover) anywhere after the trip.

sections.title: B: Change active to passive
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence in the passive.

items.sentence: People grow coffee in Brazil.
Someone damaged the school gate last night.
items.answer: The school gate was damaged last night.

items.sentence: They will announce the winners next week.
items.answer: The winners will be announced next week.

items.sentence: They don't allow phones in class.
Did they cancel the football match?
The chef prepared the food this morning.
items.answer: The food was prepared this morning.

sections.title: C: Circle the correct form
sections.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentence: This castle **visited / is visited / was visiting** by thousands of tourists each year.
The project **completed / was completed / is completing** yesterday afternoon.
The new shopping centre **will open / will be opened / is opened** next month.
The floors **are cleaned / clean / were cleaning** every night.
The windows **wasn't open / wasn't opened / didn't opened** during the storm.
**Is the parcel delivered / Is the parcel delivery / Does the parcel delivered** every morning?

sections.title: D: Complete with by or with
sections.instructions: Write by or with in each gap.

items.sentence: The story was written ...... a famous author.
The vegetables were cut ...... a sharp knife.
The school play was organised ...... the drama club.
The floor was cleaned ...... a mop.
The mural was painted ...... local artists.
The photo was taken ...... a digital camera.

sections.title: E: Phrasal verbs in the passive
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct passive form of the phrasal verb in brackets.

items.sentence: The tables ...... (put away) after the event.
All the posters ...... (put up) before the concert started.
The computers ...... (switch off) every evening.
The old stadium ...... (knock down) next year.
Her name ...... (leave out) of the competition list.
The students ...... (pick up) after the school trip.

sections.title: F: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct passive form.

items.sentence: The sandwiches **is** prepared every morning.
My bag **were** found at the station.
The invitations **will send** tomorrow.
**Is** the documents printed every day?
The classroom wasn't **decorate** for the party.
This game **play** all over the world.
Breakfast ...... in the hotel restaurant every morning.
The bridge ...... in 1965.
The awards ceremony ...... next Friday.
The messages ...... yesterday because the server crashed.
Where ...... these trainers made?
The gym ...... after the competition.

sections.title: H: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it means the same as the first.

items.sentence:
Someone washes these towels every day.
These towels ...... (WASHED) every day.

They delivered the furniture this morning.
The furniture ...... (DELIVERED) this morning.

They will publish the article next week.
The article ...... (PUBLISHED) next week.

Did someone invite Emma to the exhibition?
...... (INVITED) to the exhibition?

People don't use this classroom during the holidays.
This classroom ...... (USED) during the holidays.

Someone turned off the lights before we arrived.
The lights ...... (TURNED) before we arrived.
